content.problemStatement:
Chef has a string of lowercase English letters inputStringinputStringinputString and a list of dictionary words dictionaryWordsdictionaryWordsdictionaryWords. 

Chef wants to determine if it is possible to segment the entire string into a sequence of one or more words from the dictionary. The same dictionary word can be used multiple times in the segmentation. Help Chef decide whether such a segmentation exists.

Function DeclarationFunction NamecanSegmentStringcanSegmentStringcanSegmentString - This function determines whether the given text string can be segmented into a sequence of one or more dictionary words.

Parameters
inputStringinputStringinputString : The text string to be segmented. It consists of lowercase English letters.
dictionaryWordsdictionaryWordsdictionaryWords : A reference to a array containing the list of valid dictionary words. Each word consists of lowercase English letters.
Return Value
Returns a boolean indicating if the segmentation is possible.
truetruetrue means the string can be segmented into dictionary words; falsefalsefalse means it cannot.
Constraints
1≤T≤101 \leq T \leq 101≤T≤10
1≤∣inputString∣≤3001 \leq |inputString| \leq 3001≤∣inputString∣≤300
1≤∣dictionaryWords∣≤10001 \leq |\text{dictionaryWords}| \leq 10001≤∣dictionaryWords∣≤1000
1≤∣dictionaryWords[i]∣≤201 \leq |\text{dictionaryWords}[i]| \leq 201≤∣dictionaryWords[i]∣≤20 for each valid iii
All characters in `inputString` and `dictionaryWords[i]` are lowercase English letters.
All strings in `dictionaryWords` are unique.
constraints: All characters in `inputString` and `dictionaryWords[i]` are lowercase English letters.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a string inputString and a list of dictionary words dictionaryWords.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print 1 if the string can be segmented into one or more dictionary words, otherwise print 0.

sampleTestCases.input:
2
catsdo
cats dog sand and
pineapplepen
apple pen pineapple
sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: "catsdo" cannot be formed using any combination of the dictionary words, so output is 0. Test case 2: "pineapplepen" = "apple" + "pen" + "pineapple" (or "pineapple" + "pen"), thus output is 1.
The string "hello" can be formed using the dictionary word "hello".
The string "abcdef" can be formed using the dictionary words "ab", "cd", and "ef".

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.input:
1
abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz
a b c d e f g h i j k l m n o p q r s t u v w x y z

hidden.label: stress/general
hidden.input:
2
hello world
hello world
abcdef
ab cd ef

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using dynamic programming. We create a boolean array dp of size n+1, where n is the length of the input string. dp[i] is true if the substring from index 0 to i-1 can be segmented into dictionary words. We initialize dp[0] as true. Then, for each index i from 1 to n, we check all substrings ending at i. If a substring can be formed using a dictionary word and the substring before it can also be segmented, we set dp[i] as true.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * n^2 * m) where n is the length of the input string and m is the number of dictionary words. The reason is that for each test case, we are iterating over the string and for each index, we are checking all substrings.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n + m) where n is the length of the input string and m is the number of dictionary words. The reason is that we are storing the dp array of size n+1 and the set of dictionary words.

code.Python3:
import sys

def can_segment(s, words):
    word_set = set(words)
    n = len(s)
    dp = [False] * (n + 1)
    dp[0] = True
    for i in range(1, n + 1):
        for j in range(i):
            if dp[j] and s[j:i] in word_set:
                dp[i] = True
                break
    return dp[n]

data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split('\n')
if not data:
    sys.exit()
T = int(data[0])
idx = 1
out_lines = []
for _ in range(T):
    s = data[idx].strip(); idx += 1
    dict_words = data[idx].strip().split() if idx < len(data) else []
    idx += 1
    out_lines.append('1' if can_segment(s, dict_words) else '0')
print('\n'.join(out_lines))
